title: Chapter 111
text: OH LORD, YOU KNOW OUR FOOLISHNESS Oh Lord, You know our foolishness, And all our silly deeds; You watch us scamper here and there, Pursuing useless greeds. We sometimes doubt that You are Love, And we forget to thank; We find the Sky an empty void, The Universe a blank. We fall into despondency, And curse the hour that bore us; We either claim You don’t exist, Or else that You ignore us. So pardon us these vacant moods, Our dour and gloomy sayings; Today we own ourselves Your Fools, And celebrate by playing. We make a full acknowledgment Of all in us that’s vain – Our petty strifes and tiny woes, Our self-inflicted pain. At April Fish we jest and sing And laugh with childish glee; We puncture pomp and puffed-up pride, And smile at all we see. Your starry World’s beyond our thought And wondrous without measure; We pray, among Your Treasures bright Your Fools You’ll also treasure. From The God’s Gardeners Oral Hymnbook
